zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Fitz Rocelin於大約1151年出生在英國諾福爾可辛加姆時，她的父親William FitzRoscelin de Beaumont27歲，母親Lecia or Alicia de Beaumont42歲。她於大約1170年與Hubert III de Rie - Baron of Higham結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret Fitz Rocelin于大约1151年出生在英国諾福爾可辛加姆时，她的父亲William FitzRoscelin de Beaumont27岁，母亲Lecia or Alicia de Beaumont42岁。她于大约1170年与Hubert III de Rie - Baron of Higham结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和2个女儿的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Fitz
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
Altered form of English   Fitch  .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

variants.name: Fitzgerald
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Eamon, Liam, Aidan, Bridgid, Caitlin, Donal, Fitz, Fitzgerald, Ciaran, Colum, Dermot.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Gerald (see Gerald ), ‘son of Gerald’. This is a very common Anglo-Norman name in Ireland, where it has been Gaelicized as Mac Gearailt. Irish bearers of the name claim descent from Gerald Fitzwalter de Windsor, constable of Pembroke Castle in the early 12th century, whose grandson Raymond (or Redmond) ‘le Gros’ FitzGerald (died after 1185) led the invasion of Ireland on behalf of Strongbow in 1170. A branch of the family held the title Knight of Glin from at least 1299–2011.

variants.name: Fitzwater
meanings: Irish: altered form of Fitzwalter, a patronymic from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Walter , meaning ‘son of Walter’.

History: This is the name of a family of Norman origin, descended from Godfrey, Count of Brionne (died c. 1010). Godfrey's grandson, Richard FitzGilbert, accompanied William the Conqueror and was made Lord of Clare. His great-grandson was Robert FitzWalter, one of the barons who guaranteed the Magna Carta in 1215.

variants.name: Fitzherbert
meanings: English and Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Herbert (see Herbert ), ‘son of Herbert’.

variants.name: Fitzhenry
meanings: Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Henry (see Henry ), ‘son of Henry’. This surname is common in Ireland (mainly Connacht), where it has been Gaelicized as Mac Éinrí. In at least some cases, it seems to have been adopted as a surname indicating the illegitimate offspring of a man called Henry.

variants.name: Fitzsimmons
meanings: Irish and English: variant of Fitzsimon, with post-medieval excrescent -s, from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + either of the personal names Simon or Sigmund (see Simon ), ‘son of Simon or Sigmund’. The name is also found in the Gaelicized form Mac Síomóin.

variants.name: Fitzgibbon
meanings: Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Gibbon, a Norman French pet form of Gilbert ; ‘son of Gibbon’. This name has long been a widespread and important surname in Ireland, and Gaelicized as Mac Giobúin. Sir Gilbert (Gibbon) FitzJohn, illegitimate son of John FitzGerald, 1st Baron Desmond (died 1261), was the ancestor of a line of Anglo-Norman aristocracy in Ireland, who acquired the surname Fitzgibbon with reference to him. Gilbert and his Fitzgibbon descendants were known as ‘the White Knights’ on account of their fair complexion.

variants.name: Fitzharris
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Aileen.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Harry ‘son of Harry’. MacLysaght describes this as the name of a distinguished County Wexford family, also known as Fitzhenry .

variants.name: Fitzmaurice
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Aidan, Ciaran, Colm.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Maurice (see Morris 1), ‘son of Maurice’. This surname is common in Ireland, where it has also been Gaelicized as Mac Muiris. The Irish family of Fitzmaurice share a common Norman ancestry with the Fitzgerald family. They can be traced to Thomas Fitzmaurice, whose brother Gerald was the ancestor of the Irish Fitzgerald dynasty. Their father was Maurice Fitzgerald, a Norman baron (died 1176).

variants.name: Fitzhugh
meanings: English (Northamptonshire): from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Hugh (see Hugh ), ‘son of Hugh’.

History: William Fitzhugh (1651–1701), from Bedford, England, emigrated to VA c. 1670 and established himself on the Potomac River in what was then Stafford County, VA, as a planter and exporter. He also practiced law, was a member of the Virginia House of Burgesses, and served in 1687 as lieutenant colonel of the county militia.

variants.name: Fitzroy
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
Altered form of English   Fitch  .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

nameMeanings.name: Rocelin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Mordecai, Zalman.
Jewish (mainly from Belarus): metronymic from the Yiddish female personal name Rokhle (a pet form of the Biblical name Rachel ) + the Slavic possessive suffix -in.

variants.name: Rocklin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Avi, Hyman, Isadore.
Americanized form of Jewish   Rochlin  .
Perhaps also an Americanized form of German Röcklein, a pet form of Rock 4, or a variant of Rockel 2.

variants.name: Rachel
meanings: German (mainly Bavaria): probably a topographic name from Middle High German rach ‘rough, steep’.
German: habitational name from a place called Rachel in Bohemia.
Sorbian: from an Upper Sorbian derivative (originally also spelled Rachol) of the personal name Rach .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Fitz Rocelin was born about 1151, in Hingham, Norfolk, England, her father, William FitzRoscelin de Beaumont, was 27 and her mother, Lecia or Alicia de Beaumont, was 42. She married Hubert III de Rie - Baron of Higham about 1170. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Fitz Rocelin est née environ 1151, à Hingham, Norfolk, Angleterre, son père, William FitzRoscelin de Beaumont, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Lecia or Alicia de Beaumont, avait 42 ans. Elle a épousé Hubert III de Rie - Baron of Higham environ 1170. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret Fitz Rocelin um 1151 in Hingham, Norfolk, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William FitzRoscelin de Beaumont 27 und ihre Mutter Lecia or Alicia de Beaumont 42. Sie hat um 1170 Hubert III de Rie - Baron of Higham geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Fitz Rocelin è nata il circa 1151 nel Hingham, Norfolk, England, sua padre, William FitzRoscelin de Beaumont, aveva 27 anni e sua madre, Lecia or Alicia de Beaumont, aveva 42 anni. Lei sposato Hubert III de Rie - Baron of Higham circa 1170. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Fitz Rocelinが約1151年にイギリスノーフォルクヒンガムで生まれた時，父William FitzRoscelin de Beaumontは27歳，母Lecia or Alicia de Beaumontは42歳であった。約1170年にHubert III de Rie - Baron of Highamと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Fitz Rocelin(이)가 대략 1151년 영국 노르폴크 잉함에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William FitzRoscelin de Beaumont(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lecia or Alicia de Beaumont(은)는 42세였다. 그녀는 대략 1170년 Hubert III de Rie - Baron of Higham(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Fitz Rocelin nasceu aproximadamente 1151, em Hingham, Norfolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, William FitzRoscelin de Beaumont, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Lecia or Alicia de Beaumont, tinha 42 anos. Ela casou-se com Hubert III de Rie - Baron of Higham aproximadamente 1170. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Fitz Rocelin родилась около 1151 г. в местности хингхам, норфолк, Англия, её отцу по имени William FitzRoscelin de Beaumont было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Lecia or Alicia de Beaumont, 42. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Hubert III de Rie - Baron of Higham около 1170 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret Fitz Rocelin nació aproximadamente 1151, en Hingham, Norfolk, Inglaterra, su papá, William FitzRoscelin de Beaumont, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Lecia or Alicia de Beaumont, tenía 42 años. Se casó con Hubert III de Rie - Baron of Higham aproximadamente 1170. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas.
